data.title: Comp AI raises $2.6M pre-seed to revolutionize SOC 2 market with AI-powered automation in compliance.
data.description: Comp AI, a new company based in San Francisco, has recently obtained $2.6 million in pre-seed funding. The company focuses on revolutionizing enterprise compliance through the use of AI-powered automation. This funding will enable Comp AI to further develop their technology and expand their presence in the market.

data.title: Chippewa's Thunderstruck Logger work boot is named Boot of the Month for August, known for its durability.
data.description: In Fort Worth, Texas on August 1, 2025, the Thunderstruck 9" Waterproof Nano Comp Toe Logger by Chippewa has been awarded the title of Boot of the Month for August. This boot, designed for individuals who value durability and high performance from their footwear, comes in two tough color options: charcoal black.

data.title: Vornado Realty to release quarterly earnings on 2025-08-04. Analysts expect EPS of $0.21. Past quarters show beats, +3.68% in share price. Stock trading at $38.42, up 37.84% in 52 weeks. Market sentiment and guidance also crucial for investors.
data.description: Vornado Realty is set to release its quarterly earnings report on August 4, 2025, with analysts predicting an EPS of $0.21. In the previous quarter, the company beat EPS estimates by $0.11, resulting in a 3.68% increase in share price. The company's past performance shows a history of beating EPS estimates, with corresponding positive price changes. As of July 31, Vornado Realty shares were trading at $38.42, marking a 37.84% increase over the last 52 weeks. Analysts suggest that investor focus should be on the company's guidance for the next quarter, which can impact stock prices more significantly than earnings beats or misses. Understanding market sentiments and expectations is crucial for investors ahead of the earnings release.

data.title: Viper Energy to release Q2 earnings on Aug 4. Analysts expect EPS of $0.36. Shares trading at $37.66, down 7.61% in 52 weeks. Bearish sentiment among long-term shareholders. Analysts rate as Outperform.
data.description: Viper Energy is set to release its quarterly earnings report on August 4, 2025. Analysts expect the company to report an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.36. In the previous quarter, Viper Energy beat EPS estimates by $0.07, causing a 3.48% drop in share price the next day. Over the last year, Viper Energy's shares have decreased by 7.61%, leading to bearish sentiments among long-term shareholders. The company's past performance saw varying price changes based on earnings results. Market reactions are often influenced by guidance for future quarters rather than just earnings performance. Analysts rate Viper Energy as Outperform, based on 15 analyst ratings. Investors are eagerly waiting for the earnings report to see if the company surpasses estimates and provides positive guidance.

data.title: KBC Group remains well-capitalized in 2025 EU-wide EBA stress test results. View slides at kbc.com.
data.description: KBC has acknowledged the recent announcements by the European Banking Authority (EBA) concerning the outcomes of the 2025 EU-wide stress test. Additional details on the KBC-specific data can be found on the company's website at www.kbc.com.

data.title: Analysts increase forecasts for Illumina after Q2 earnings of $1.19/share (vs. $1.01 estimate) and sales of $1.059 billion (vs. $1.049 billion estimate). CEO credits strong growth in clinical segment.
data.description: Illumina, Inc. disclosed its second-quarter earnings, surpassing expectations. The company reported $1.19 per share earnings for the quarter, exceeding the anticipated $1.01 per share. Additionally, quarterly sales stood at $1.059 billion, surpassing the predicted $1.049 billion. CEO Jacob Thaysen attributed the success to the strong performance in X consumables and rapid growth in the clinical segment, their largest customer base. In the research realm, Illumina continues to support its customers effectively.

data.title: European Banking Authority releases 2025 stress test results for Societe Generale.
data.description: In the recent data released, it was indicated that the unemployment rate has risen to 6.2%, the highest level in the past five years. The number of individuals who lost their jobs reached 1.5 million, an increase of 800,000 compared to the previous year. Furthermore, the economy experienced a decrease of 3% in the GDP growth rate, marking a significant decline compared to the projected 1.5% growth. This downturn in economic indicators has raised concerns among experts and policymakers regarding the need for immediate intervention to address the rising unemployment and economic contraction.

This information provides stakeholders with insight into the company's structure and decision-making capabilities.

data.title: L’Oréal releases 2025 half-year financial report on Aug 1, 2025, filed with Autorité des marchés financiers.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, L’Oréal disclosed its half-year financial report for the period ending on June 30, 2025. The report has been published for public access and submitted to the Autorité des marchés financiers.

data.title: Avocados from Peru donates 100,000 avocados to San Diego Food Bank on National Avocado Day, benefiting 500,000 servings.
data.description: In San Diego, the Jacobs & Cushman San Diego Food Bank, a leading hunger-relief organization, revealed a substantial contribution on National Avocado Day. Avocados from Peru generously donated 100,000 avocados during the 3rd annual Guac-Off Challenge. This donation will facilitate over 500,000 servings of nutritious food to disadvantaged families in San Diego County. The Food Bank serves these families through a network of 450 nonprofit partners.

data.title: BTC reaches ATH monthly close, Trump spooks markets, altcoins lead fall. SEC launches 'Project Crypto,' most not securities. Coinbase earnings mix, stock down 10%. Tether profits $4.9bn in Q2. ETH treasuries hit $10b. Stablecoins now 18th holder of US treasuries. Visa integrates EURC, PYUSD, USDG. USDC, CCTP V2 coming to Hyperliquid. Mill City Ventures acquires $278m SUI. HK starts stablecoin licensing.
data.description: Bitcoin recently achieved an all-time high monthly close before experiencing a decline in value. Altcoins led the sell-off in the crypto market following a period of mixed earnings. The SEC launched 'Project Crypto' aimed at tokenizing assets, with a focus on assets that are not considered securities according to Atkins. A strategy report indicated a profit of $10 billion despite funding costs. Coinbase reported mixed earnings, resulting in a 10% decrease in its stock value. Coinbase is planning to expand its services to include tokenized Real World Assets (RWAs) and stocks.Additionally, Tether announced profits of $4.9 billion in the second quarter. The treasuries of Ethereum reached $10 billion. The Ethereum Foundation unveiled a 10-year plan to achieve a transaction speed of 10,000 transactions per second. Stablecoins now rank as the 18th largest holder of US treasuries. Jamie Dimon expressed support for stablecoins but not Bitcoin. There have been amendments to the S-1 form for the SOL ETF. Visa has integrated EURC, PYUSD, and USDG into its services. USDC and CCTP V2 are set to be introduced on Hyperliquid by Circle.In other news, Mill City Ventures made a $278 million purchase of SUI. Hong Kong has initiated a stablecoin licensing regime to regulate operations within the territory.
2025 half-year financial report for Planisware now available.
European banks weather a stress test that simulates trade shock.

data.title: Invesco closed-end funds declared dividends, with key dates: ex-date 8/18/2025, payable date 8/29/2025.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, the Board of Trustees for the Invesco closed-end funds announced dividends for their shareholders. The dividends are set to be paid on August 29, 2025, for those holding shares as of the record date, which is also August 18, 2025. The reinvest date is August 29, 2025, and the ex-date is set for August 18, 2025. Shareholders of the mentioned closed-end management investment company can expect monthly payouts as per the schedule provided.

data.title: Cineverse partners with Moviefone to bring back phone line, fans can call 1-802-377-FILM to hear Peter Dinklage's voice as The Toxic Avenger.
data.description: Popular movie companies have come together to revive the Moviefone phone line, inviting fans to dial 1-802-377-FILM to engage with the latest cinema releases before the highly anticipated August 29 premiere. This initiative signifies the return of a beloved movie resource and is led by Cineverse (Nasdaq: CNVS), a cutting-edge entertainment studio based in Los Angeles.
